coll: I (Coptic I, Syriac not detected): [first two folios: Vat. sir. 647, ff. 27–28], Syriac 64, ff. 1–6 ; II (Coptic not detected): Syriac 64, ff. 7–14 ; III (Coptic not detected): ff. 15–24 ; IV (Coptic not detected): ff. 25–32 ; V (Coptic not detected): ff. 33–40 ; VI (Coptic not detected): ff. 41–48 ; VII (Coptic not detected): ff. 49–56 ; VIII (Coptic not detected): ff. 57–64 ; IX (Coptic not detected): ff. 65–72 ; X (Syriac: traces, Coptic not detected): ff. 73–80 ; XI (Coptic XII, Syriac not detected): ff. 81–88 ; XII (Coptic not detected): ff. 89–96 ; XIII (Coptic XIII: traces): ff. 97–104 ; XIV (Coptic XIV): ff. 105–112 ; XV (Coptic XV): ff. 113–120 ; XVI (Coptic XVI, traces): ff. 121–128 ; XVII (Coptic not detected): ff. 129–136 + f. 137a (an insertion glued to f. 136v) ; XVIII (Coptic XVIII): ff. 137b–144 ; <XIX> (Syriac/Coptic not detected): ff. 145–152 ; XX (Coptic XX): ff. 153–155, [Vat. sir. 647, f. 43], Syriac 64, ff. 156–158

related_mss.label: Originally belonged together with the following

note: Palimpsest throughout with undertext in Greek (GK)
Black and red ink are used in the text and in ornamentation
Traces of ruling with hard point marking borders of the columns and individual lines (direction: vertical)

excerpt.as_written: (sic!) ܥܠ ܚܝܠܐ ܕܡܪܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ ܢܟܬܘܒ ܐܪܡܘܠܐܘܢ
translation: With the help of Our Lord Jesus Christ we are writing Heirmologion

excerpt.as_written:
ܩܐܛܐ̄ ܕܦܨܚܐ ܩ ܐ̄ ܝܘܡܐ ܗܘ ܕܩܝܡܬܐ ܬܘ ܟܠܢ ܡܗ̈ܝܡܢܐ ܢܦܪܓ ܒܚܓܐ
ܦܨܚܐ ܗܘ ܓܝܪ ܕܡܪܝܐ ܦܨܚܐ. ܡܢ ܡܘܬܐ ܓܝܪ ܠܘܬ ܚ̈ܝܐ
note: '= Ἀναστάσεως ἡμέρα λαμπρυνθῶμεν Λαοί, Πάσχα Κυρίου, Πάσχα· ἐκ γὰρ θανάτου πρὸς ζωήν (S. Eustratiadēs, Εἱρμολόγιον, Chennevierès-sur-Marne, 1932, akolouthia 6 (hereafter: EE 6), ode 1, p. 4)

excerpt.as_written: ܡܘܪܒܝܢܢ ܠܟܝ ܟܠܢ. ܐܡܗ ܕܢܘܗܪܐ ܫܪܝܪܐ. ܘܡܩܠܣܝܢܢ ܪܒܬ. ܝܠܕܬ ܐܠܗܐ ܒܬܘ̄. ܐܟܡܢ ܕܝܠܕܬܝ ܠܦܪܘܩܐ ܕܢܦܫܬܐ ܕܟܠܢ
note: '= Μεγαλύνομέν σε τὴν μητέρα τοῦ φωτὸς καὶ δοξάζομέν σε, Θεοτόκε Παρθένε, ὡς σωτῆρα τεκοῦσαν τῶν ψυχῶν ἡμῶν. (EE 326, ode 9, p. 298)

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).

program.label: Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1
program.description: Digitized under the Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1 (2018-2023), a collaboration of St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, with funding from the Arcadia Fund, the Ahmanson Foundation, and the Steinmetz Family Foundation. The project provided high-resolution images and basic description of the Arabic and Syriac manuscripts held in the Monastery’s library.
